Title: Weather Report - Live
Comment:
I know this is a long one - but I hope you can give it your time. It’s a patient song that grows & rewards with a euphoric end. 
Shinji Sato, the vocalist died shortly after this performance. He was just 33. He poured so much passion into EVERYTHING and you can feel it in all their live songs - esp Long Season. Their music allows me to float away for a moment and just heal and enrich. I love life + music. This song means a lot to me and I understand if it’s not your thing but hope you like 🫶🏽

I highly recommend watching the music video to this, it’s phenomenal. I wish FlyLo got more recognition 🙏🏽

Title: Vogue
Comment: In French, “genre fluide” means both gender-fluid and genre-fluid. No song captures both senses as well as Madonna’s Vogue. It helped popularize the ballroom culture and underground voguing scene of NYC in late 80s/early 90s. The song blends house, disco, and dance-pop.  It’s diverse in genre because it’s representing a diverse scene.

Title: Ting Tun Up Part II

Title: I'll Take Care of U

I picked this song because most of you will probably recognize this is the sample Drake used for Take Care and I think his vocals cut against the piano that makes it sort of mash two contrasting sounds into the same song.

Title: Tank!
Comment: Cowboy Bebop was born from the unadulterated creative freedom of TV in the 90s. It's such a rare example of a fusion of styles in storytelling, animation, music, and voice acting, mixing what were totally unrelated elements in a way that was so perfectly orchestrated that they feel as if they had always existed in harmony, with jazz at the foundation. I can't recommend this show enough— it's a such unique piece of collaborative art and such a stylish piece of pop culture.

Title: COLORATURA
Comment:
帆波の揺蕩へ
天つ日民追ひ風と
Ichiko explains the idea behind the song: “a pirate ship with a tattered sail. And this ship might not even be visible to the human eye, but it has cargo, and maybe the listeners themselves are the cargo. As we travel across the sea, there’s a violent storm and the waves are raging—they’re as high as the ship itself. At some point, the ship goes into the waves and goes underwater, but it is here—underneath the surface—that we see these luminescent creatures.”

Title: Porcelain

Title: Letting Go
Comment:
Angie is bi, which makes this the perfect week to share the song that will undoubtedly be at the top of my Spotify wrapped this year. The lyrics, the build, the motion of the instrumental post-chorus, the feral yelling at the end - I adore everything about this song and it gives me chills every single time.

Also if, like me, you’re hooked by her voice - I think her vocals really shine in her earlier Salt album. Highly recommend 👌🏻

Title: ARE WE STILL FRIENDS?
Comment: Tyler exists in a genre that's been historically homophobic, but he’s always been so confident and unapologetic that when he started coming out, it just felt like an extension of who he already was. Always found his music to be inspiring because of that. Igor is an album about loving a boy who's already in love with a girl. Probably my favorite song off the list.

Title: Sofia
Comment: Bisexual queen and a sapphic anthem. If you downvote her, you hate bi women and I will screenshot the comments and send them to your place of employment.

Title: Don't Stop Me Now - Remastered 2011
Comment: Don't hate, master bait

Title: Shut up (and Sleep with Me)
Comment: Written and produced in the relative LGBTQ+ safety of mid 90s Germany, this was the most garish song I'd ever heard when it hit North American airwaves. It put a big dent in the cultural armour of sexual taboos. And if this Euro pop classic winds up as a downvote collector with y'all; no tea, no shade.

Title: All Along the Watchtower
Comment: I made a 60sec music video in Mr. Candy's sophomore computer animation class starring little chiclets from the flash game Avalanche acting out the lyrics. It had some sweet shots, like an opening orbit shot around a watchtower, a hand swirling a glass of wine, and a moon-silhouetted Hendrix. It ended up getting a lot of love from people, which was nice since it was one of the first things I really poured my heart into, and feeling others recognizing that was a new experience for me.

Title: Last Voyage
Comment:
In Spiritfarer you help people come to terms with their own death. Once you’ve helped them accomplish everything they want to in life, this song plays and they move on to the next

The summer I played that game, my grandma passed away. I hummed this song as I held her hand in the hospital. She was 90 and she loved me

I know this is a plainly emotional play for your heart strings, but this song means a lot to me and I stopped caring about points a month ago. I love my grandma and I love you too

Title: Brains Brawn and Beauty
Comment: This song is by my dad. He tried to make it as a rock star in the 70s, but the Soviet Union feared his vibes and prevented his rise to stardom. So, he took up a stable job to support his young son's comic book addiction, before returning to music thereafter. The memory associated with this song is, when he was playing on stage and his friend yelled, "Glen! Looks like retirement is going great!" To which he replied, "I didn't retire; I just changed careers." Then he shredded this sweet jam.

Title: Lisztomania
Comment: This was the first song on my iPod Nano on my middle school trip to Germany/Switzerland. It played all the time. Me and my best friend would sit next to each other on the EF Tours bus and share my earphones bumping to it. It brings back visions of farms and cuckoo clocks and the Swiss alps. And the real irony is that only recently I learned that “Lisztomania” means a need to listen to music all the time (based on the composer Franz Liszt). That’s how I felt about this song :’)

Title: Time to Pretend
Comment: December 2013 - my first semester at college and our first club Xmas party. This song played and I remember cheesing like I never had before. I made a wonderful group of friends that for the first time in my life, was because of shared passions and knew would be in my life forever. And also really the first time I felt like I was being my authentic self around people. I’m always transported back to that moment when I hear this song. It felt like my life was just beginning. (I knowww sappy)

Title: Jigga What / Faint
Comment: Somewhat unbelievably got tickets to big outdoor gig in the summer with both Jay-Z and Linkin Park on the play bill. They came out separately and then left us waiting for like an hour before they eventually came out together and played this as their opener. First ever time in a moshpit. Absolutely insane atmosphere. Core memory

Title: Let It Be - Remastered 2015
Comment: Gunna get sappy here. My dad has an extensive record collection and I grew up with him spinning The Beatles, EW&F, Santana, etc. He would always play “Let It Be” when we were going through hard times as a family. When our pets died, when we lost a job, when my grandmother died when I was 15… I associate it with leaning on family and loved ones when times are tough, and that you are not alone in your grief or sadness.

Title: Clarity
Comment: This song brings me back to the summer of 2022, when the whole family was together at the farmhouse. I remember myself running through cornfields with the dog, and watching the best sunset I’ve ever seen from the barn window (Uff, happy tears)

Title: Home
Comment: En andra svensk låt. Heard this on my way home after a morning red eye in June 2017. Was on the NJ Transit on my way into NYC on a Friday afternoon. Was heading to Long Island to surprise my dad for his 60th birthday party. Was at least an hour out when I heard this but home was very clearly calling.

Title: mona lisa
Comment:
First heard this song at Jig/Erikas the night I met many of you!

I was in a pretty bad way when I moved to SC, and spent most of this day curled in a ball in bed convinced no one would like me. But I got there and everyone was so friendly and kind and welcoming. u guys had no idea how much that meant🥺 so when I hear this song I remember sitting on the livingroom floor, this bright happy tune floating in from the kitchen speaker, and feeling like someone had plugged the aux cord into my heart💛

Title: Could You Be Loved
Comment: I remember my mom being obsessed with this song and dancing to it ever Sunday morning. My dad, to this day, is still sick of this song

Title: Just like Heaven
Album: Kiss Me, Kiss Me, Kiss Me
Comment:
It’s summer, the windows are down and I am riding in the car with Dad. The warm air rushes in and whips my hair around, and the sky is the kind of blue you don’t notice until it’s gone.
He’s drumming the steering wheel with his fingers, tapping out the rhythm before Robert Smith even starts singing. The road curves & the trees open up to that first glimpse of the lake. He turns up the volume and smiles at me, the smile he reserves for his favorite things. “This one,” he says, “this one is magic”
